title: Fix MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline to Handle Nested Fields

statement.markdown: Write a function that takes an array of MongoDB aggregation pipeline stages as input and returns the modified pipeline that handles nested fields. The function should recursively traverse the pipeline stages, replace any occurrences of `$project` with a new stage that uses the `includeNested` option to include nested fields.
statement.inputFormat: An array of MongoDB aggregation pipeline stages, where each stage is an object with `type` and `spec` properties
statement.notes: The `includeNested` option should be set to true for the `$project` stage to include nested fields.

examples.input: [{ type: 'project', spec: { a: 1, b: 1 } }, { type: 'match', spec: { c: 1 } }]
examples.output: [{ type: 'project', spec: { a: 1, b: 1 }, includeNested: true }, { type: 'match', spec: { c: 1 } }]
examples.explanation: The `$project` stage is modified to include nested fields

examples.input: [{ type: 'project', spec: { a: 1, b: 1 }, includeNested: true }, { type: 'match', spec: { c: 1 } }]
examples.output: [{ type: 'project', spec: { a: 1, b: 1 }, includeNested: true }, { type: 'match', spec: { c: 1 } }]
examples.explanation: No change needed for this pipeline stage

hints.text: Start by iterating over the pipeline stages
Check if the current stage is a `$project` stage
If it is, modify the stage to include nested fields

editorial.title: Handling Nested Fields in MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline
editorial.intuition: The pipeline stages should be recursively traversed to replace any occurrences of `$project` with a new stage that uses the `includeNested` option
editorial.bruteForce: This approach can be time-consuming and may not be efficient for large pipelines
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient approach would be to use a recursive function to traverse the pipeline stages
editorial.optimalSolution: This approach uses a recursive function to traverse the pipeline stages and modify any `$project` stages to include nested fields
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The modified pipeline stages will correctly include nested fields for the `$project` stage
editorial.dryRun: Example usage of the function with a sample pipeline
commonMistakes: Not recursively traversing the pipeline stages
Not modifying the `$project` stage to include nested fields

testCases.input: [{ type: 'project', spec: { a: 1, b: 1 } }, { type: 'match', spec: { c: 1 } }]
testCases.output: [{ type: 'project', spec: { a: 1, b: 1 }, includeNested: true }, { type: 'match', spec: { c: 1 } }]

testCases.input: [{ type: 'project', spec: { a: 1, b: 1 }, includeNested: true }, { type: 'match', spec: { c: 1 } }]
testCases.output: [{ type: 'project', spec: { a: 1, b: 1 }, includeNested: true }, { type: 'match', spec: { c: 1 } }]
